info.title: Containarium API
info.description: Container management API for LXC-based development environments. Provides both gRPC and REST interfaces for managing containers, SSH keys, and system resources.

post.summary:
ReportContainerState records the calling host's observed state
for one of its assigned containers. The cloud uses this to
surface the user-facing containers.state field and (in a future
slice) to react to FAILED reports with reassignment.
post.description:
Errors:
  - InvalidArgument: malformed container_id, empty / unknown state.
  - NotFound: no live assignment for that container belongs to
    the calling host. Single error code for "container unknown"
    and "container is on a different host" — the calling host
    can't distinguish, by design.
  - Unauthenticated: missing / invalid host-bearer.

parameters.name: containerId
parameters.description:
The container the host is reporting about. The host must hold a
live container_assignments row for this container; cross-host
reports collapse to NotFound for probe resistance (the calling
host can't tell whether the container exists on another host).

post.summary:
EnrollHost redeems a single-use join token and registers the BYO host.
Self-authenticating via the token in the body (NOT host-bearer — the row
doesn't exist yet).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
EnrollHostRequest carries the single-use join token a BYO host redeems to
register itself with the cloud (BYO-compute report path). Unlike every other
ActuationService RPC this one is NOT host-bearer authed — the host row
doesn't exist yet; the cloud validates + single-use-redeems the token.

post.summary:
Heartbeat updates hosts.last_heartbeat_at for the calling host.
A stale gap (>2 × the host's expected interval) marks the host
for the HostSweeper, which reassigns its containers. Hosts are
expected to call this every N seconds with N << the staleness
threshold.
post.description:
Auth: requires a valid `host-bearer` header. Returns
Unauthenticated if missing / malformed / tombstoned. The
method is otherwise idempotent.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
HeartbeatRequest is intentionally empty for v1. The host's identity
comes from the bearer; there's nothing else the cloud needs to
know to bump last_heartbeat_at. Future fields (current observed
state, host-side health flags) are additive.

post.summary:
ReportHostStatus upserts the calling host's capability profile +
`doctor` self-check and bumps its heartbeat. Requires a valid
`host-bearer` header. Idempotent.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
ReportHostStatusRequest is a BYO host's push of its self-measured
capability profile + `doctor` self-check. Host-bearer authed; the host id
comes from the bearer, never the body.

get.description: Returns all packaged agent skills that can be run individually or composed into a crew.
Returns one skill's definition, including its agent card, allowed scopes, and allowed peers.

post.description: Provisions the skill's box, mints a scoped token, runs one task, and returns the artifact.

post.summary: Send a task to a peer agent (A2A)
post.description: Delegates a task to a running peer agent over the agent-to-agent transport and returns the peer's artifact.

parameters.name: toPeerId
parameters.description: The target peer to deliver the task to.

get.description: Returns all custom alert rules stored in the database.

post.description: Creates a new custom alert rule. The rule is persisted in PostgreSQL and synced to vmalert for evaluation.

get.description: Returns the built-in default alert rules for system and container monitoring. These rules are always active and cannot be modified.
Returns a single alert rule by its ID.

delete.description: Deletes an alert rule by ID. The rule is removed from both the database and vmalert.

put.description: Updates an existing alert rule. Changes are synced to vmalert automatically.

get.description: Returns a list of deployed applications with optional filtering by state or username.

parameters.name: stateFilter
parameters.description:
Filter by state

 - APP_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state (should not be used)
 - APP_STATE_UPLOADING: Source code is being uploaded
 - APP_STATE_BUILDING: Container image is being built (using Podman)
 - APP_STATE_RUNNING: App is running and healthy
 - APP_STATE_STOPPED: App is stopped by user
 - APP_STATE_FAILED: Build or runtime failure
 - APP_STATE_RESTARTING: App is restarting

post.description: Deploys a new application or updates an existing one. Accepts source code + Dockerfile/Containerfile or auto-detects language and generates one.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific application including state, domain, and configuration.

delete.description: Permanently deletes an application and optionally removes associated data volumes.

get.description: Returns application logs with optional streaming. Use follow=true for real-time log streaming.

post.description: Restarts an application by stopping and starting it.
Starts a stopped application.
Stops a running application. The application can be started again later.

get.description: Lists every backend in the fleet (local daemon + tunnel peers) with health, version, OS, running container count, and GPU inventory. Admin-only — discloses fleet topology.

post.summary: Trigger daemon upgrade on a backend
post.description: Upgrades a backend's daemon to the binary the sentinel currently serves, immediately instead of on the periodic tick. SHA-verified and smoke-tested before an atomic swap; the daemon restarts. Admin-only. Returns an upgrade_id for GetUpgradeStatus.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
TriggerUpgradeRequest asks a backend to upgrade its daemon binary now instead
of waiting for the periodic auto-update tick. The daemon pulls the binary the
sentinel currently serves (SHA-verified), smoke-tests it, swaps atomically
(keeping the previous binary as .old), and restarts. See #354 Phase B.

get.description: Lists stored backup records (newest first), optionally filtered by tenant.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description:
Optional tenant filter. Empty lists backups for all tenants
(admin view).

post.description: Runs pg_dump inside the tenant's container and stores the dump at the chosen off-host destination (local backup dir or GCS).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
CreateBackupRequest dumps a container's database and stores it at the
chosen destination.

get.description: Returns the metadata for a single stored backup.

delete.description: Deletes a stored dump and its metadata. Retention enforcement is the caller's responsibility.

post.description: Streams a stored dump back into a container's database via pg_restore. Destructive when clean=true.

get.description: Returns a backend's last-recorded capability profile (hardware class, GPU probe, micro-benchmark, region) without re-running it. Admin-only.

parameters.name: backendId
parameters.description: Backend to read. Empty = the local/primary daemon's own host.

post.description: Records a backend's capability profile (hardware class, GPU probe, bounded CPU/memory micro-benchmark, region) and reconciles measured vs self-reported class. Surfaced through ListBackends. Admin-only.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
ProfileBackendRequest asks a backend to (re)record its capability profile:
read system info, run the GPU passthrough probe and the bounded
CPU/memory micro-benchmark, derive the measured class, and persist it. See
#681.

get.description: Returns this backend's current headroom advertisement and recomputed spare figures. Admin-only.

delete.description: Withdraws this backend's headroom advertisement. Idempotent — succeeds even when nothing is currently advertised. Admin-only.

parameters.name: drain
parameters.description:
When true the backend also reclaims the guest workloads it had implicitly
offered, draining them gracefully within a bounded window instead of
hard-killing them. Each drained workload is stopped through the normal
stop path so the control plane observes a stop event and can reschedule.
When false (default) withdraw only flips the advertisement off and leaves
running guests untouched. See #682.

parameters.name: drainWindowSeconds
parameters.description:
Bounded wall-clock budget (seconds) for the graceful drain. Once exhausted
any still-running candidate is force-stopped so the host is reliably
reclaimed (no wedge on repeated advertise/withdraw cycles). 0 applies the
backend default (120s). Only honored when drain is true.

post.description: Publishes this backend's spare scheduling headroom (spare CPU/memory/disk + host idle fraction) to the control plane, bounded by a local policy. Surfaced through ListBackends. Admin-only.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
AdvertiseCapacityRequest publishes (or republishes) this backend's spare
scheduling headroom to the control plane, bounded by the supplied policy.
See #680.

get.description: Returns a list of containers with optional filtering by state, username, or labels. Use query parameters to filter results.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description:
Filter by state (optional, empty means all states)

 - CONTAINER_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state (should not be used)
 - CONTAINER_STATE_RUNNING: Container is running
 - CONTAINER_STATE_STOPPED: Container is stopped
 - CONTAINER_STATE_FROZEN: Container is frozen (paused)
 - CONTAINER_STATE_CREATING: Container is being created
 - CONTAINER_STATE_ERROR: Container creation failed or is in error state
 - CONTAINER_STATE_PROVISIONING: Container is running but provisioning (installing stack/packages)

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Filter by username (optional, empty means all users)

parameters.name: labelFilter
parameters.description: Filter by labels (optional, empty means no filter)

post.description: Creates a new LXC container with specified resources and configuration. The container will be started automatically after creation.

get.description: Returns active network connections for a container tracked by conntrack.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description:
Filter by protocol (optional)

 - PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified protocol (should not be used)
 - PROTOCOL_TCP: TCP protocol
 - PROTOCOL_UDP: UDP protocol
 - PROTOCOL_ICMP: ICMP protocol

parameters.name: destIpPrefix
parameters.description: Filter by destination IP prefix (optional, e.g., "10.100.")

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of connections to return (default: 100)

get.description: Returns aggregate connection statistics for a container.
Returns aggregated traffic statistics over time for analysis.
Returns historical connection data from persistent storage.

post.summary: Protect or unprotect a container from automated deletion
post.description: Sets the container's delete policy (#284). DELETE_POLICY_PROTECTED makes the ttlsweeper auto-reap and `containarium prune` skip the box (e.g. a persistent runner); DELETE_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED returns it to the default (eligible for both). A deliberate single-box delete always succeeds regardless.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Container name — the bare username, matching the per-container RPC
convention (the handler appends "-container"; see SetContainerTTL).

post.summary: Schedule or clear a container's auto-delete TTL
post.description: Stamps the container with a wall-clock auto-delete time consumed by the daemon's TTL sweeper. duration_seconds=0 clears any existing TTL; > 0 sets it to now()+duration. Capped at 604800 (7 days). Used by the containarium-run GitHub Action to auto-clean failed-CI debug boxes.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Container name (e.g. "alice-container"). Matches the URL path
segment used by other per-container RPCs.

get.description: Returns all collaborators with access to a container.

post.description: Adds a collaborator to a container. The collaborator can SSH via ProxyJump and sudo su to the container owner with full session logging for audit trail.

delete.description: Removes a collaborator's access to a container, deleting both jump server and container accounts.

get.description: Returns detailed information about a specific container including state, resources, network info, and current metrics.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container to get

delete.description: Permanently deletes a container. Use force=true query parameter to delete running containers.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container to delete

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force delete even if container is running

get.description: Returns the network ACL (firewall rules) configured for a user's DevBox container.

put.description: Updates the network ACL for a DevBox container. Use a preset for common configurations or specify custom rules.

post.description: Called by the source daemon during MoveContainer after the LXC has been Incus-copied to this host. Registers the container's host-side accounts and route record so it's fully under Containarium's control. Not intended for direct operator use.

post.summary: Enable or disable auto-sleep on a container
post.description: Writes the per-container auto-sleep opt-in metadata (Incus user.* keys). Does not itself stop the container — use stop_container for that. Phase 2 (the actual idle-tick) and Phase 3 (wake-on-HTTP) consume this flag.
Frees disk space inside a container by removing temporary files, package manager caches, and trimming journal logs. Useful when disk is full and resize fails.

get.description: Inspects backend-local state for the container: container runtime state, host /etc/passwd entry, whether the shell wrapper exists, and recent sshd journal lines matching the username. Returns a structured diagnosis with likely_cause and ordered next_actions for the caller to apply.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container to debug

post.summary: Install a software stack on a container
post.description: Installs a pre-configured software stack or base script on an existing running container.

post.summary: Enable or disable OTel monitoring on a container
post.description: Live-flip OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT and related env vars on an existing container and restart it so the change reaches the app. Use this to retrofit monitoring onto containers created before --monitoring was wired into create_container.

Pre-copy snapshot-based migration to a peer daemon. Sub-second downtime on ZFS/btrfs storage with low write rate; up to minutes on dir-pool or active workloads. Caller must have incus remotes configured both directions between the source and target hosts.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description:
Username of the container to move (required). Must exist on the
local daemon — moving a container the caller doesn't own returns
PermissionDenied.

put.description: Dynamically adjusts CPU, memory, and disk resources without container restart. Disk can only be increased, not decreased.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container to resize

post.description: Adds an SSH public key to a container's authorized_keys file, allowing SSH access with the corresponding private key.

delete.description: Removes an SSH public key from a container's authorized_keys file, revoking SSH access for that key. The ssh_public_key should be URL-encoded.

parameters.name: sshPublicKey
parameters.description: SSH public key to remove (exact match)

post.description: Starts a stopped container. The container must exist and be in STOPPED state.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container to start

post.description: Gracefully stops a running container with optional timeout and force parameters. The container can be started again later.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container to stop

get.description: Returns the state, trace id, and (when complete) the artifact of a crew execution.
Returns all crews (collaborating skill sets) available to run.

post.description: Provisions each skill's box, gates A2A traffic with network policy compiled from allowed_peers, runs the collaboration, and returns the run handle.

get.description: Returns one crew's definition, including its topology and member skills.
Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for real-time resource updates. Filter by resource types using query parameters.

parameters.name: resourceTypes
parameters.description:
Filter by resource types (empty = all types)

 - RESOURCE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified resource type
 - RESOURCE_TYPE_CONTAINER: Container resource
 - RESOURCE_TYPE_APP: App resource
 - RESOURCE_TYPE_ROUTE: Route resource
 - RESOURCE_TYPE_METRICS: Metrics resource
 - RESOURCE_TYPE_TRAFFIC: Traffic resource

parameters.name: includeMetrics
parameters.description: Include metrics events (can be noisy, default false)

parameters.name: metricsIntervalSeconds
parameters.description: Metrics interval in seconds (default: 5, min: 1, max: 60)

get.summary: Get a backend's signed integrity self-measurement
get.description: Computes and signs a fresh integrity self-measurement (daemon binary digest, loaded in-kernel program object digests, policy/config-state digest) with the node's identity key. The daemon also emits this on its heartbeat. Admin-only.

parameters.name: backendId
parameters.description:
Backend to measure. Empty = the local/primary daemon's own host; a peer id
forwards to that peer (which measures itself).

get.summary: Count secrets by encryption mode (legacy vs envelope)
get.description: Reports total / legacy / envelope row counts. When legacy=0 the deployment is fully migrated. Admin + kms:admin scope.

post.summary: Re-wrap legacy secrets under the active KMS KEK
post.description: Batches legacy rows and rewrites them to envelope form, verifying each round-trips before committing. dry_run walks + verifies without writing. Admin + kms:admin scope.

get.summary: Report the active KMS backend and envelope-retirement state
get.description: Returns the configured backend (none|inproc|vault|gcp|aws), a human-readable description, whether a real KMS is active, and whether CONTAINARIUM_REQUIRE_ENVELOPE is set. Admin + kms:admin scope.
Returns runtime metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network usage) for containers. Specify username to get metrics for a specific container, or omit to get all containers.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container (empty for all containers)

get.description: Returns runtime metrics (CPU, memory, disk, network usage) for containers. Specify username to get metrics for a specific container, or omit to get all containers.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Username of the container (empty for all containers)

get.description: List every tenant's network-isolation policy. Admin-only.

post.description: Create or replace a tenant's network-isolation policy (validated + normalized). Admin-only.
Atomically add/remove a tenant's virtual-patch deny rules (#660). Admin-only.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
PatchNetworkPolicyDenyRulesRequest atomically mutates a tenant's virtual-patch
deny rules (#660): the server reads the current rules, removes any whose CIDR
is in remove_cidrs, adds/replaces the `add` rules (deny rules are keyed by
CIDR — an add for an existing CIDR replaces it), re-normalizes, and writes
back under a lock, so concurrent edits don't lose updates. The allow-policy
(egress, mode, intra, metadata) is untouched. The normalized stored policy is
echoed back.

get.description: Return a tenant's network-isolation policy. Admin-only.

delete.description: Remove a tenant's network-isolation policy (idempotent). Admin-only.

get.description: List every operator-managed exploit signature (#661). Admin-only.

post.description: Create or replace a global cleartext exploit signature (#661). Admin-only.

delete.description: Remove an operator-managed exploit signature (#661, idempotent). Admin-only.

get.description: Returns available firewall rule presets with their default configurations.
Returns DNS records configured for the base domain. Useful for selecting available subdomains when creating routes.
Returns all TCP/UDP port forwarding rules configured via iptables. These routes provide direct passthrough without TLS termination.

post.description: Creates a new TCP/UDP port forwarding rule via iptables. Traffic is forwarded directly to the container without TLS termination, suitable for mTLS or custom protocols.

delete.description: Removes a TCP/UDP port forwarding rule from iptables.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description:
Protocol: TCP or UDP

 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified protocol (defaults to HTTP)
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_HTTP: HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 (standard web traffic) - for proxy routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_GRPC: gRPC (requires HTTP/2 transport) - for proxy routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_TCP: TCP passthrough - for passthrough routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_UDP: UDP passthrough - for passthrough routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_TLS_PASSTHROUGH: TLS passthrough - SNI-based routing without TLS termination (for mTLS gRPC)

put.description: Updates an existing TCP/UDP port forwarding rule. Can be used to enable/disable the route or change the target.

parameters.name: externalPort
parameters.description: External port to update (required, identifies the route)

get.description: Returns all DNS/domain to container mappings configured in the reverse proxy.

post.description: Creates a new DNS/domain to container mapping in the reverse proxy.

delete.description: Removes a DNS/domain to container mapping from the reverse proxy.

put.description: Updates an existing DNS/domain to container mapping.

get.description: Returns the network topology including all containers, apps, proxy routes, and their connections for visualization.
Returns the current penetration test configuration including enabled modules and external tool availability.
Returns security findings from penetration test scans with optional filtering by severity, category, and status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of findings to return (default: 50)

get.description: Returns aggregate statistics of security findings including counts by severity and category.

post.description: Upgrades the OS package that contains the vulnerable binary to the latest version. Only works for Trivy container findings where the binary belongs to an installed package.
Marks a finding as suppressed with a reason. Suppressed findings are excluded from open counts and alerts.
Triggers an on-demand penetration test scan across all registered endpoints and containers.

get.description: Returns recent penetration test scan runs with their status and finding counts.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of scan runs to return (default: 20)

parameters.name: containerName
parameters.description:
Optional: filter to scan runs scoped to this container. Empty =
return all runs (both cluster-wide and container-scoped).

get.description: Returns details of a specific penetration test scan run including finding counts.

post.description: Downloads and installs an external pentest tool (nuclei or trivy) from GitHub releases.

get.description: Returns all built-in recipes that can be deployed as dedicated containers.
Returns a zero-click bootstrap URL for an agent-workspace box, embeddable in the console to authenticate the in-box workspace UI seamlessly.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
The box identity (the container is named "<name>-container"), same scheme
as DeployRecipeRequest.name.

get.description: Returns the definition of a single recipe, including its parameters, ports, and resource defaults.

post.description: Provisions a new dedicated container (with optional GPU passthrough), runs the recipe's image inside it, and exposes the configured ports.

get.description: Returns the latest Containarium release tag from GitHub (cached) alongside this daemon's running version and whether an update is available.

post.summary: Create or update a tenant secret
post.description: Encrypts the value with the daemon's master key and stores it in Postgres. Idempotent: same (username, name) bumps version. The value is never logged. Name must match ^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$ up to 128 chars; value max 64 KiB.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
SetSecretRequest creates or updates a tenant secret. Idempotent —
repeated calls with the same (username, name) bump the version and
replace the value.

get.description: Returns name/version/timestamp tuples. Values are only readable via GetSecret per-name (audit-logged).

post.summary: Re-stamp tenant secrets into the LXC env
post.description: Reads all of the tenant's secrets from the DB, decrypts, and updates the LXC's environment.<NAME> config keys to match. Running processes keep their old env (POSIX); new execs see the refreshed values.

get.description: Returns the decrypted plaintext value. Audit-logged. Caller's responsibility to keep the value safe once it's on the wire.

delete.description: Removes the secret from Postgres. Does NOT cascade to env-var stamps on running containers — call RefreshSecrets to re-stamp without restarting.

get.description: Returns ClamAV scan reports with optional filtering by container, status, and date range.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of reports to return (default: 50)

post.description: Triggers an on-demand ClamAV scan for a specific container or all running user containers.

get.description: Returns a summary of ClamAV scan status across all containers including last scan time and infection status.
Returns recent scan jobs and counts by status (pending, running, completed, failed).
Returns all configured software stacks including their parameters (name, type, default, etc.) for rendering the Create Container form.
Returns alerting system status including vmalert and Alertmanager health, webhook configuration, and rule counts.

put.description: Updates the alerting system configuration, such as the webhook URL for notifications. Changes take effect immediately.

get.description: Returns the history of webhook delivery attempts including success/failure status, HTTP codes, and timing.

post.description: Sends a test alert notification to the configured webhook URL with current system status information.

get.description: Returns information about the host system including Incus version, available resources, and container counts.
Returns monitoring configuration including Grafana and VictoriaMetrics URLs. Used by the web UI to embed the Grafana dashboard.

post.summary:
Disable stops the autostart unit. Does NOT stop the running
containers — use the compose CLI for that.

Discover compose stacks under the tenant's LXC. Walks from the
tenant's $HOME (or supplied `root`) up to `max_depth`, skipping
common noise directories.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Tenant LXC to discover stacks in. Required.

post.summary:
Enable installs the systemd-user autostart unit for a compose
stack and brings it up. Idempotent — force=true refreshes the
unit when the compose file has changed.

parameters.name: username
parameters.description: Tenant LXC to operate in. Required.

get.summary:
Status returns ComposeStack metadata for a single dir without
a full filesystem walk. Cheaper than Discover when the caller
already knows the path.

post.summary: Exchange a refresh token for a new (access, refresh) pair
post.description: Validates the refresh token (signature + exp + tt='refresh' + not revoked), mints a new short-lived access token, mints a new long-lived refresh token, and revokes the input refresh token's jti. Refresh tokens are single-use; the new refresh token must be stored by the client for the next exchange.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
Phase 1.6 part B — exchange a refresh token for a new
(access, refresh) pair. Rotates the refresh token:
the prior jti is revoked on success, so each refresh
token is single-use. Replay = denied + audit signal.

post.summary: Revoke a JWT by its jti
post.description: Adds the token's jti to the revocation list. The token will be rejected on the next request that tries to use it. Admin-only. Idempotent — revoking an already-revoked jti is a no-op (the original revocation reason is preserved).

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: RevokeTokenRequest names a token to invalidate.

get.description: Returns revocation rows ordered most-recent-first. Default scope is non-expired revocations only; pass include_expired=true for forensic enumeration. Admin-only with the tokens:write scope.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Max rows to return. 0 → 100 default. Server caps at
1000.

parameters.name: includeExpired
parameters.description:
When true, include rows whose expires_at has already
passed (default false — most operators only care
about active revocations).

parameters.name: jtiPrefix
parameters.description:
Narrow by jti prefix. Empty disables. Useful when an
operator only remembers part of a leaked jti.

get.description: Opens a Server-Sent Events stream for real-time connection events.

parameters.name: eventTypes
parameters.description:
Event types to include (empty = all types)

 - TRAFFIC_EVENT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified event type
 - TRAFFIC_EVENT_TYPE_NEW: New connection established
 - TRAFFIC_EVENT_TYPE_UPDATE: Connection state or counters updated
 - TRAFFIC_EVENT_TYPE_DESTROY: Connection terminated

get.description: Returns the status of an upgrade started by TriggerUpgrade. Note: a local self-upgrade restarts the daemon and drops job state, returning 'unknown' afterward; compare the backend version in ListBackends to confirm.

post.description: Launches a throwaway nvidia.runtime LXC on the backend, runs nvidia-smi inside, and returns whether the GPU is usable (status, model, driver). Admin-only.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
ValidateGPURequest asks a backend to prove GPU passthrough works from inside
an LXC. See #316.

get.description: Lists shared volumes and reports shared_volumes_supported for this backend (true only when a CephFS pool exists).

parameters.name: pool
parameters.description: Optional pool filter. Empty lists the detected CephFS pool.

post.description: Creates a CephFS custom (filesystem) volume that can be attached read-write to multiple containers. Rejected on non-CephFS hosts.

parameters.name: force
parameters.description: Force deletion even if the volume is still attached to containers.

post.summary: Attach a shared volume to a container
post.description: Mounts the CephFS volume into the container at mount_path. Multiple containers may attach the same volume RW concurrently.

post.summary: Detach a shared volume from a container

get.description: Returns ZAP security alerts with optional filtering by risk level and status.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of alerts to return (default: 50)

get.description: Returns aggregate statistics of ZAP alerts including counts by risk level.

post.description: Marks a ZAP alert as suppressed with a reason.

get.description: Returns the current ZAP scanner configuration and availability status.

post.description: Downloads and installs OWASP ZAP scanner.
Triggers an on-demand OWASP ZAP scan across all exposed endpoints.

get.description: Returns recent ZAP scan runs with their status and alert counts.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of scan runs to return (default: 20)

parameters.name: containerName
parameters.description:
Optional: filter to scan runs scoped to this container. Empty =
return all runs (both cluster-wide and container-scoped).

get.description: Downloads a ZAP scan report in the specified format (html, json, csv).

ACLAction.description:
- ACL_ACTION_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified action (should not be used)
 - ACL_ACTION_ALLOW: Allow the traffic
 - ACL_ACTION_DROP: Drop the traffic silently
 - ACL_ACTION_REJECT: Reject the traffic with ICMP response
ACLAction.title: ACLAction represents the action to take when a rule matches

ACLPreset.description:
- ACL_PRESET_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified preset
 - ACL_PRESET_FULL_ISOLATION: Full isolation: only allow HTTP from proxy, block inter-container traffic
 - ACL_PRESET_HTTP_ONLY: HTTP only: allow HTTP/HTTPS inbound, standard egress
 - ACL_PRESET_PERMISSIVE: Permissive: allow all traffic (for development)
 - ACL_PRESET_CUSTOM: Custom: user-defined rules
ACLPreset.title: ACLPreset represents predefined firewall rule sets

defaultIngressRules.title: Default ingress rules for this preset

defaultEgressRules.title: Default egress rules for this preset

action.title: Action to take when rule matches

ACLRule.title: ACLRule represents a single firewall rule

AccessType.description:
- ACCESS_TYPE_SSH: SSH access (default for Linux containers)
 - ACCESS_TYPE_RDP: RDP access (for Windows VMs, via Guacamole browser-based client)
AccessType.title: AccessType indicates how to connect to an instance

sshPublicKey.description:
SSH public key for the collaborator.
Deprecated: prefer ssh_public_keys. Kept for back-compat — when
ssh_public_keys is empty this single key is used.

grantSudo.title: Grant full sudo access (not just su - owner)

grantContainerRuntime.title: Add to docker/podman group for container runtime access

sshPublicKeys.description:
SSH public keys for the collaborator — all are authorized, so a
collaborator with several machines can connect from any of them.
Takes precedence over ssh_public_key when non-empty. See #369.

AddCollaboratorBody.title: AddCollaboratorRequest is the request to add a collaborator to a container

sshCommand.title: SSH connection command for the collaborator

AddCollaboratorResponse.title: AddCollaboratorResponse is the response from adding a collaborator

protocol.title: Protocol: TCP or UDP (defaults to TCP)

AddPassthroughRouteRequest.title: AddPassthroughRouteRequest adds a new passthrough route

containerName.title: Optional: Associated container name (for display purposes)

protocol.title: Protocol type (HTTP or gRPC, defaults to HTTP)

AddRouteRequest.title: AddRouteRequest adds a new proxy route

AddSSHKeyBody.title: AddSSHKeyRequest is the request to add an SSH key to a container

totalKeys.title: Total number of SSH keys now authorized

AddSSHKeyResponse.title: AddSSHKeyResponse is the response from adding an SSH key

sourceRoutes.description:
The source daemon's record of which routes belonged to this
container, so the destination can recreate them at its own
container IP. Each entry is "<full-domain>|<port>|<protocol>".
Empty for stateless containers with no exposed ports.

AdoptMigratedContainerBody.description:
AdoptMigratedContainerRequest is sent by the source daemon's
MoveContainer to the destination daemon AFTER `incus copy` has
completed and the LXC exists on the destination's incusd. The
destination then registers the container with its own Containarium
state (host user account, route record at the new IP).

Internal use only — exposed via REST for daemon-to-daemon coordination
over the same PeerPool channel used by other forwarding RPCs.

newIpAddress.description:
The migrated container's new IP address on the destination's
incusbr0. The source uses this for the final route store update.

AdoptMigratedContainerResponse.description:
AdoptMigratedContainerResponse confirms the destination registered
the container and returns its IP for the source's route swap.

policy.description:
The local policy that bounds the advertisement. When omitted the backend
applies its current/default policy (always-open window, no exclusions, no
reservation).

AdvertiseCapacityRequest.description:
AdvertiseCapacityRequest publishes (or republishes) this backend's spare
scheduling headroom to the control plane, bounded by the supplied policy.
See #680.

AdvertiseCapacityResponse.description:
AdvertiseCapacityResponse returns the headroom the backend computed and is
now advertising. When the policy window is closed, advertised is true but
the spare figures are zero.

outputJson.description: JSON output matching the peer's agent_card output schema.

error.description: Human-readable error when state is FAILED.

AgentArtifact.description: AgentArtifact is the result a peer agent returns for a task.

id.description: Stable handle used by peers to address this agent (e.g. "researcher").

description.description: What this agent does — surfaced to peers and to a planner.

capabilities.description:
Free-form capability tags a planner can match against (e.g.
"web-search", "code-review", "summarize").

inputSchemaJson.description: JSON Schema describing the task input this agent accepts.

outputSchemaJson.description: JSON Schema describing the artifact this agent returns.

AgentCard.description:
AgentCard is the discovery document a skill serves so peer agents can find
it and learn how to delegate to it (A2A, Phase 1). It is carried in-proto so
the wire format stays insulated from upstream A2A churn.

recipeId.description: Reference an existing recipe by id (e.g. "agent-runtime").

recipe.description: Inline recipe definition for one-off skills.

systemPrompt.description: System prompt / persona handed to the model inside the box.

allowedScopes.description:
Platform MCP scopes this agent is permitted to use (e.g.
"containers:read", "containers:create"). Validated against the scope
taxonomy in internal/auth/scopes.go; minted into the agent's JWT at
launch. Least-privilege: a skill gets ONLY what it declares.

agentCard.description: A2A discovery document this skill serves (Phase 1).

allowedPeers.description:
IDs of the OTHER skills this skill is permitted to send A2A tasks to.
In Phase 2 this list compiles to an eBPF NetworkPolicy egress allowlist,
so an agent literally cannot open a socket to a non-allowed peer (enforced
in-kernel, not in prompt). A skill that is only allowed one peer cannot
reach any other. An empty list = talks to no peer (leaf agent). Inert
until Phase 2.

model.description:
Model identifier the runtime should drive this agent with (e.g.
"claude-opus-4-8"). Empty = runtime default.

AgentSkill.description:
AgentSkill is a packaged, runnable agent: a Recipe (the box — image,
resources, GPU) plus the agent layer (who it is, what tools it may call,
who it may talk to). This is the unit `containarium agent run` launches.

Key invariant: allowed_scopes and allowed_peers are typed, first-class
fields — NOT a prompt string. allowed_scopes is minted into the agent's JWT
at launch; allowed_peers compiles into eBPF network policy (Phase 2). The
typing IS the security boundary.

AgentTaskState.description: AgentTaskState tracks the lifecycle of a delegated A2A task.

name.title: Human-readable name for the alert rule

duration.title: Duration the expression must be true before firing (e.g. "5m")

description.title: Human-readable description of what this alert means

labels.title: Additional labels attached to the alert

createdAt.title: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the rule was created

updatedAt.title: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the rule was last updated

AlertRule.title: AlertRule represents an alerting rule evaluated by vmalert

id.title: Unique app ID (UUID) - immutable primary key

containerName.title: LXC container name where app runs

errorMessage.title: Error message if state is FAILED

aclPreset.title: Firewall ACL preset applied to this app

AppResources.title: AppResources defines resource limits for an app container

AppState.description:
- APP_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state (should not be used)
 - APP_STATE_UPLOADING: Source code is being uploaded
 - APP_STATE_BUILDING: Container image is being built (using Podman)
 - APP_STATE_RUNNING: App is running and healthy
 - APP_STATE_STOPPED: App is stopped by user
 - APP_STATE_FAILED: Build or runtime failure
 - APP_STATE_RESTARTING: App is restarting
AppState.title: AppState represents the lifecycle state of an application

BackendGPU.description: BackendGPU describes one GPU on a backend.

type.description: Backend kind: "local" for this daemon, "tunnel" for a peer.

healthy.description: Whether the backend is currently healthy / reachable.

version.description: Containarium daemon version running on this backend (e.g. "0.21.0").

uptimeSeconds.description: Seconds this backend has been up (local backend only).

lastSeenAt.description: RFC3339 timestamp this backend was last seen (peers).

containerCount.description: Number of containers currently running on this backend.

headroom.description:
Spare scheduling capacity this backend currently advertises to the
control plane, or null when nothing is advertised (withdrawn or never
published). Lets the control plane direct scheduling at hosts that have
explicitly offered freed headroom. See #680.

capabilityProfile.description:
The backend's hardware capability profile recorded at join (hardware
class, a bounded CPU/memory micro-benchmark, and region), or null when no
profile has been recorded yet. Lets the control plane place work by
measured class rather than self-reported class alone. See #681.

BackendInfo.description:
BackendInfo describes one backend in the fleet — the local daemon
plus any tunnel-connected peers. Emitted by ListBackends (GET
/v1/backends). The field shape is the wire contract the CLI and MCP
clients consume; it supersedes the hand-coded /v1/backends handler
this RPC replaced (see #354).

BackupDestination.description:
BackupDestination is where a logical dump is stored *off* the database
host. The whole point of the feature is getting the data off-box, so a
destination is always required — there is no "stay on the same disk"
option (a dump that shares a failure domain with the database it came
from is not a backup; see docs/DB-BACKUP-OPERATIONS.md).

 - BACKUP_DESTINATION_UNSPECIFIED: Unset — the daemon rejects a create with this value.
 - BACKUP_DESTINATION_LOCAL: A backup directory on the daemon host, distinct from the container's
own data disk. Survives container/disk loss but NOT host loss — use
for dev or as a staging tier in front of an off-host copy.
 - BACKUP_DESTINATION_GCS: A Google Cloud Storage bucket, written via the host's `gcloud`
(`gcloud storage cp`). True off-host durability; the recommended
production destination.

id.description:
Stable identifier: "<username>-<database>-<UTC timestamp>"
(e.g. "alice-app-20260605T130405Z"). Used by Get/Restore/Delete.

username.description: Tenant (username) whose container's database was dumped.

database.description: Logical database name that was dumped.

createdAt.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp of when the dump completed.

sizeBytes.description: Size of the compressed custom-format dump, in bytes.

sha256.description: SHA-256 of the dump bytes, for integrity verification on restore.

location.description:
Concrete location of the dump: a host path for LOCAL, a gs:// URI
for GCS.

engine.description: Database engine. "postgres" is the only engine in v1.

BackupRecord.description:
BackupRecord is the metadata index entry for one stored dump. The dump
itself lives at `location`; this record is persisted as a small JSON
sidecar in the daemon's backup directory so `ListBackups` works without
a database dependency (the thing we are backing up may itself be down).

cpuOpsPerSec.description:
CPU score: integer-work iterations completed per second during the bounded
run (higher is faster). Single-threaded.

memBytesPerSec.description:
Memory score: bytes per second moved through a bounded buffer copy/sum
loop (higher is faster).

durationMs.description:
Wall-clock duration of the benchmark in milliseconds (bounded; the runner
caps each phase).

CapabilityBenchmark.description:
CapabilityBenchmark is a lightweight, time-bounded CPU/memory micro-benchmark
run when the profile is recorded. The scores are relative integrity/capacity
signals, not absolute hardware specs — they let the control plane confirm a
backend's self-reported class is plausible. See #681.

cpuCores.description: Total physical CPU cores on the host (from SystemInfo.total_cpus).

cpuModel.description:
Best-effort CPU model/generation string (e.g. "AMD EPYC 7B13"), empty when
the host doesn't expose one.

gpuModel.description:
GPU model name from the existing nvidia.runtime passthrough probe
(ValidateGPU), empty on a CPU-only backend.

gpuDriverVersion.description: GPU driver version from the same probe, empty on a CPU-only backend.

gpuAvailable.description: Whether the GPU passthrough probe saw a usable GPU.

region.description:
Region the backend serves (operator-set via --region; falls back to the
pool name). Empty when unset.

reportedClass.description:
Self-reported hardware class the operator assigned the backend (e.g. the
pool name / declared tier), if any.

measuredClass.description:
Measured hardware class derived from the profile (e.g. "gpu", "cpu-large",
"cpu-small"). Coarse buckets; the control plane refines placement from the
raw figures.

classConsistent.description:
True when measured_class matches reported_class (or reported_class is
empty, treated as consistent). False flags a drift the control plane and
operator should reconcile.

benchmark.description: The bounded micro-benchmark result. See CapabilityBenchmark.

profiledAt.description: RFC3339 timestamp the profile was recorded.

CapabilityProfile.description:
CapabilityProfile is a backend's hardware capability snapshot recorded at
join (and refreshable on demand): the hardware class derived from the host's
system info + GPU probe, a lightweight bounded CPU/memory micro-benchmark,
and the region. The control plane reads it through the backend surface to
place work by measured capability rather than by the self-reported class
alone, and to detect a backend whose measured class drifts from what it
reports. See #681.

advertised.description:
Whether an advertisement is currently active. False means the backend has
withdrawn (or never published) — the rest of the fields are then a stale
snapshot the control plane must ignore.

spareCpus.description: Spare CPU cores the backend offers for control-plane-directed scheduling.

spareMemoryBytes.description:
Spare memory in bytes (derived from SystemInfo.available_memory_bytes,
bounded by policy headroom reservation).

spareDiskBytes.description:
Spare disk in bytes (derived from SystemInfo.available_disk_bytes,
bounded by policy headroom reservation).

idleFraction.description:
Host-level idle fraction in [0,1]: the share of running user containers
currently idle (a host aggregate over the per-container idle signal). A
high value indicates a box that would scale down and can instead offer
its headroom. 0 when no signal is available.

advertisedAt.description: RFC3339 timestamp the advertisement was last (re)published.

policy.description: The local policy that bounds this advertisement.

CapacityHeadroom.description:
CapacityHeadroom is a backend's explicit, policy-bounded offer of spare
scheduling capacity to the control plane. Today a backend's capacity is
implicit (derived from system info); a box that would otherwise scale down
can instead publish its freed headroom here so the control plane can direct
work at it. Surfaced through BackendInfo (ListBackends). See #680.

windowStartHour.description:
Local-clock hour the advertisement window opens (0-23). When
window_start_hour == window_end_hour the window is always open.

windowEndHour.description:
Local-clock hour the advertisement window closes (0-23, exclusive). May be
less than window_start_hour to express an overnight window (e.g. 22..6).

excludedWorkloadClasses.description:
Workload classes that must NOT be displaced or co-scheduled — running
containers carrying any of these classes are excluded from the idle/spare
computation, so a host running protected work never advertises it as free.
Matched against the container's "user.containarium.workload_class" label.

reserveFraction.description:
Fraction of host resources to hold back as a safety reservation, in
[0,1). Spare = available * (1 - reserve_fraction). 0 means offer all
available headroom.

CapacityPolicy.description:
CapacityPolicy bounds what a backend is willing to advertise. The
advertisement only computes spare capacity when the current time falls
inside the allowed window and respects the excluded workload classes.
Operator-set per backend. See #680.

lastScanAt.title: When the last scan was performed

lastFindingsCount.title: Number of findings in the last scan

infectedScans.title: Number of scans that found infections

backendId.title: Backend ID identifying which node owns this container

findings.title: Infected file lines from ClamAV output

scannedAt.title: When the scan was performed (ISO 8601 timestamp)

createdAt.title: When the report was created in the database

backendId.title: Backend ID identifying which node performed the scan

ClamavReport.title: ClamavReport represents a single ClamAV scan result for a container

CleanupDiskBody.title: CleanupDiskRequest is the request to clean up disk space in a container

message.title: Human-readable summary of what was cleaned

CleanupDiskResponse.title: CleanupDiskResponse is the response from cleaning up disk space

accountName.title: Full account name on jump server and container (e.g., "alice-container-bob")

sshPublicKey.title: SSH public key for this collaborator

addedAt.title: Unix timestamp when collaborator was added

hasSudo.title: Has full sudo access (not just su - owner)

hasContainerRuntime.title: Has docker/podman group membership for container runtime access

Collaborator.title: Collaborator represents a user with access to a container

composeDir.description: Absolute path to the compose directory inside the LXC.

composeFile.description:
Absolute path to the compose file itself (compose.yml /
docker-compose.yml — whichever exists).

composeBin.description:
The compose binary the agent-box resolved on PATH:
"podman-compose" | "docker compose" | "podman compose" | "" (none).

composeModifiedAt.description:
RFC3339 mtime of the compose file. Useful for "is the unit
stale vs the compose file" diagnostics.

runningCount.description:
Number of services currently in running state. 0 when compose_bin
is empty or the call failed.

totalCount.description:
Total services declared in the compose file. 0 on resolution
failure.

autostartEnabled.description:
True if the systemd-user autostart unit for this stack is
installed and enabled.

unitModifiedAt.description:
RFC3339 mtime of the autostart unit file. Empty when the unit
doesn't exist. Useful to detect "I re-ran enable but the unit
hasn't been refreshed" drift.

ComposeStack.description:
ComposeStack is the public-safe metadata for one discovered compose
directory inside a tenant's LXC. Mirrors the agent-box-side struct
of the same name (internal/agentbox/compose.go).

`running_count` + `total_count` are deliberately NOT a single
boolean: agents need to distinguish fully-up / partial-degradation
/ fully-down to decide whether to alert vs. restart vs. ignore.
"running" matches docker compose's State="running" and podman's
equivalent; "total" is every service declared in the compose file
regardless of state.

containerName.title: Container name this connection belongs to

firstSeen.title: When the connection was first seen

lastSeen.title: When the connection was last active

Connection.title: Connection represents an active or recent network connection

ConnectionState.description:
- CONNECTION_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state
 - CONNECTION_STATE_NEW: New connection (SYN sent/received)
 - CONNECTION_STATE_ESTABLISHED: Connection established
 - CONNECTION_STATE_RELATED: Related connection (e.g., FTP data connection)
 - CONNECTION_STATE_TIME_WAIT: Connection in TIME_WAIT state
 - CONNECTION_STATE_CLOSE_WAIT: Connection in CLOSE_WAIT state
 - CONNECTION_STATE_FIN_WAIT: Connection in FIN_WAIT state
 - CONNECTION_STATE_CLOSED: Connection closed
 - CONNECTION_STATE_SYN_SENT: SYN sent, waiting for response
 - CONNECTION_STATE_SYN_RECV: SYN received, waiting for ACK
ConnectionState.title: ConnectionState represents the state of a TCP connection

topDestinations.title: Top destination IPs by connection count

ConnectionSummary.title: ConnectionSummary provides aggregate statistics for a container

resources.title: Resource limits applied to this container

sshKeys.title: SSH public keys authorized for this container

createdAt.title: Unix timestamp when container was created

updatedAt.title: Unix timestamp when container was last updated

labels.title: Custom labels for categorization and metadata

image.title: Image used to create the container (e.g., "images:ubuntu/24.04")

stack.title: Software stack installed in the container (e.g., "nodejs", "python")

gpuDevice.description:
GPU device info (e.g., GPU ID or PCI address if attached). For a
multi-GPU container this is the first device; see `gpu_devices` for all.

backendId.title: Backend ID this container runs on (e.g., "gcp-spot", "tunnel-node-a-gpu")

osType.title: Operating system type of the container

accessType.title: How to connect to this instance (SSH for Linux, RDP for Windows)

rdpAddress.title: RDP connection address (e.g., "10.100.0.50:3389") — populated for Windows VMs

monitoringEnabled.description:
Whether application-emitted OpenTelemetry is enabled for this
container (mirrors the --monitoring flag passed at creation).
When true, the LXC has OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT and related
env vars stamped on it. Read-only — set at create time;
changing requires recreate (a ToggleMonitoring RPC may come
later, but doesn't exist in v1).

pool.description:
Pool tag of the backend hosting this container (e.g., "prod",
"demo", "lab"). Mirrors the peer's --pool registration tag and
is the discriminator used for placement and per-pool routing
(such as base-domain selection). Empty for untagged backends.

autoSleepEnabled.description:
Whether the container is opted into manual auto-sleep tracking
(Phase 1 of the serverless feature). Read-only; toggled via
ToggleAutoSleep. Backed by the user.containarium.auto_sleep_enabled
Incus config key.

idleThresholdMinutes.description:
Idle minutes before the Phase 2 auto-sleep daemon would stop
the container. Defaults to 15 when unset. Backed by the
user.containarium.idle_threshold_minutes Incus config key.

ttlExpiresAt.description:
Wall-clock time at which the daemon's TTL sweeper will delete
this container. Unset = no TTL, container persists indefinitely.
Set via SetContainerTTL. Consumed by internal/ttlsweeper which
runs once a minute and force-deletes any container whose
ttl_expires_at has elapsed (with a small clock-skew grace
margin). Used by the containarium-run GitHub Action's
"keep on failure" path to schedule auto-deletion of debug boxes.

sshHost.description:
- direct mode  → the container's reachable IP (same value as
    network.ip_address);
  - sentinel-jump mode → the configured sentinel's public host,
    e.g. "region-a.example.com".

Combined with `username`, this is the connect target
`username@ssh_host`. Empty means "no explicit host — fall back to
network.ip_address". Clients (the ssh_config generator, a future
`connect` verb) should USE this verbatim rather than reconstructing
a host from the IP / sentinel config themselves.
sshHost.title:
The host:port a client should SSH to in order to reach this
container, as computed by the daemon from its own routing config —
so a client never has to infer the SSH target from other fields:

stoppedAt.description:
Two-phase reaping status (#525), read from the Incus config the daemon
stamps. Lets a reader see where a box is in the stopped→delete lifecycle
without host access (#264 fleet hygiene) — alongside ttl_expires_at (the
absolute death date) and auto_sleep_enabled (idle→stop).

stopped_at: when the box most recently became STOPPED (cleared on start,
so a woken box has none). The stopped→delete timer runs from here. Unset
when running / never stopped.

deleteAfterStoppedSeconds.description:
delete_after_stopped_seconds: the box's stopped→delete window. > 0 means
it auto-deletes once it has been stopped that long (reclaiming disk); 0 =
never delete on stop. Opt-in, independent of auto_sleep_enabled.

deletePolicy.description:
Whether this box is protected from the daemon's automated/bulk deletion
paths (#284). DELETE_POLICY_PROTECTED means the ttlsweeper auto-reap and
`containarium prune` skip it; a deliberate single-box delete still removes
it. Read from the user.containarium.delete_policy Incus config key; set via
SetContainerDeletePolicy. Absent/unprotected = DELETE_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED.

gpuDevices.description:
All GPU devices attached to this container (device index or PCI address
per entry). For a single-GPU container this has one entry mirroring
`gpu_device`; empty when no GPU is attached.

Container.title: Container represents a complete container instance

cpuUsageSeconds.title: CPU usage in seconds (cumulative CPU time used)

networkRxBytes.title: Network bytes received (all interfaces except loopback)

networkTxBytes.title: Network bytes transmitted (all interfaces except loopback)

processCount.title: Number of running processes in container

ContainerMetrics.title: ContainerMetrics contains runtime metrics for a container

ContainerState.description:
- CONTAINER_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified state (should not be used)
 - CONTAINER_STATE_RUNNING: Container is running
 - CONTAINER_STATE_STOPPED: Container is stopped
 - CONTAINER_STATE_FROZEN: Container is frozen (paused)
 - CONTAINER_STATE_CREATING: Container is being created
 - CONTAINER_STATE_ERROR: Container creation failed or is in error state
 - CONTAINER_STATE_PROVISIONING: Container is running but provisioning (installing stack/packages)
ContainerState.title: ContainerState represents the current state of a container

CreateAlertRuleRequest.title: CreateAlertRuleRequest creates a new alert rule

CreateAlertRuleResponse.title: CreateAlertRuleResponse returns the created alert rule

username.description: Tenant (username) whose container holds the database.

connection.description: Connection parameters for pg_dump inside the container.

gcsBucket.description:
For GCS: the destination bucket/prefix, e.g. "gs://my-backups/pg".
Ignored for LOCAL. The object key is appended as "<id>.dump".

CreateBackupRequest.description:
CreateBackupRequest dumps a container's database and stores it at the
chosen destination.

resources.title: Resource limits (optional, uses defaults if not specified)

sshKeys.title: SSH public keys to authorize (optional, can be added later)

image.title: Container image to use (optional, defaults to images:ubuntu/24.04)

enablePodman.title: Enable Podman support (optional, defaults to true)

async.title:
Async mode - if true, returns immediately with CREATING state
Client should poll GetContainer to check completion

staticIp.title:
Static IP address to assign (optional, e.g., "10.0.3.100")
Must be within the container network CIDR (e.g., 10.0.3.0/24)
If empty, DHCP will assign an IP automatically

stack.title:
Software stack to install (optional, e.g., "nodejs", "python", "fullstack")
See available stacks in configs/stacks.yaml

gpu.description:
Deprecated: single-GPU field. Prefer `gpus`. When `gpus` is non-empty it
takes precedence; when `gpus` is empty a non-empty `gpu` is treated as a
single-element list.
gpu.title: GPU device ID for passthrough (e.g., "0" for first GPU, PCI address, or empty for none)

backendId.title: Target backend ID for creation (empty = primary backend)

osType.title: Operating system type (takes precedence over image when set)

stackParameters.description:
Stack parameters — passed to install scripts as env vars
(CONTAINARIUM_STACK_<key>). Set by the web UI's Create Container form
based on the selected stack's declared parameters. Optional.

monitoring.description:
Enable application-emitted OpenTelemetry. When true, the daemon
stamps the container with OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT and
related env vars pointing at the core OTel collector, so any
OTel SDK inside the container ships telemetry to the platform's
VictoriaMetrics without app-side configuration. Default false —
opt-in matches the "platform doesn't move data unless told to"
principle and avoids surprise telemetry from prototype
workloads. The daemon's own cgroup-level metrics for the
container are independent of this flag and continue regardless.

pool.title:
Pool selector — when set and backend_id is empty, the daemon
picks any healthy peer tagged with this pool

gitSource.description:
Git source provisioning — when git_source is set, the daemon
fetches the repo into the box's workspace at create time (via
incus exec, no caller→box SSH). Removes the caller's need to
push code in over rsync/scp. See
prd/oss/create-git-source.md (Containarium-cloud).

git_source: clone URL, e.g. "https://github.com/org/repo".

gitRef.description:
git_ref: the exact ref to check out — full SHA (preferred,
reproducible), branch, tag, or a server ref like
"refs/pull/<n>/merge". Empty = the remote's default branch.

workspacePath.description:
workspace_path: where in the box to place the source. Empty
defaults to "/workspace".

ttlSeconds.description:
Birth TTL (optional) — seconds from creation after which the box is
auto-deleted. When > 0, the daemon stamps ttl_expires_at = now() +
ttl_seconds at create time (same persistence as SetContainerTTL), so
the box is born with a death date and the ttlsweeper reaps it even if
the client dies right after create — no separate `ttl set` call needed
(#523). 0 = no TTL (today's behavior). Capped at 604800 (7 days);
larger values return InvalidArgument, same bound as SetContainerTTL.

idleStopMinutes.description:
Birth idle-stop (optional) — minutes of inactivity after which the box
is auto-STOPPED (freeing CPU/RAM, keeping disk; wakes on next access).
When > 0, the daemon enables auto-sleep at create time with this idle
threshold (same persistence as ToggleAutoSleep), so the box is born
with the stop timer too — no separate `toggle_auto_sleep` call to
forget (#524, the stop half of #522's default-sleep→default-dead
model; ttl_seconds is the delete half). 0 = no auto-sleep (today's
behavior). The auto-sleep loop treats an open SSH/exec session as
active, so an actively-debugged box is never stopped mid-session.

deleteAfterStoppedSeconds.description:
Birth stopped→delete (optional) — seconds the box may remain STOPPED
before it is auto-deleted, reclaiming disk after idle_stop_minutes
already reclaimed CPU/RAM (#525, the second timer of #522's two-phase
lifecycle). The clock runs from the stop transition and RESETS when the
box is woken (accessed), so a box someone keeps investigating is never
reaped; only one left continuously stopped past this window is. This is
a SEPARATE opt-in from idle_stop_minutes / auto-sleep: a scale-to-zero
box that merely sleeps must never be deleted just for being stopped.
0 = never delete on stop (today's behavior).

gpus.description:
GPU device IDs for passthrough — one entry per GPU to attach (device
index like "0"/"1" or PCI address like "0000:0b:00.0"). Supersedes the
singular `gpu` field: when non-empty `gpu` is ignored; when empty a
non-empty `gpu` is treated as a single-element list. Each device is
resolved to a stable PCI address at create time.

CreateContainerRequest.title: CreateContainerRequest is the request to create a new container

sshCommand.title: SSH connection string for the user (Linux containers)

CreateContainerResponse.title: CreateContainerResponse is the response from creating a container

sizeBytes.description:
Quota in bytes. Required (> 0) so a shared scratch space can't grow
unbounded on the cluster.

pool.description: CephFS pool. Empty = the daemon's detected default CephFS pool.

description.description: Short description of the task purpose.

skillIds.description:
Skill ids that make up this crew (in pipeline order when PIPELINE;
coordinator first when ORCHESTRATOR).

Crew.description: Crew is a collaborating set of skills bound to a task purpose.

traceId.description: Shared trace id threaded through every agent's audit entries for this run.

inputJson.description: JSON input handed to the crew's entry skill(s).

artifactJson.description: JSON artifact produced by the crew. Populated when state is COMPLETED.

error.description: Human-readable error when state is FAILED.

CrewRun.description:
CrewRun is one execution of a crew. Every A2A hop and MCP call made by any
agent in the run is audit-logged under this run's trace_id, so a run is
itself an evidence artifact.

CrewRunState.description: CrewRunState tracks the lifecycle of a single crew execution.

CrewTopology.description:
CrewTopology describes how a crew's skills are wired together.

 - CREW_TOPOLOGY_PIPELINE: Linear pipeline: output of skill[i] feeds skill[i+1].
 - CREW_TOPOLOGY_ORCHESTRATOR: A coordinator skill fans tasks out to worker skills and synthesizes.
 - CREW_TOPOLOGY_FREEFORM: Skills coordinate freely over A2A within the allowed_peers graph; the crew
just bounds the set and the trace.

data.title: Record data (IP address for A records)

containerState.title:
Container runtime state from the daemon's view
("running", "stopped", "missing", "error: <reason>")

hostUserExists.title: Whether a host-level Linux user with this username exists

hostUserShell.title: The shell column from /etc/passwd for this user (empty if no user)

hostUserShellExists.title: Whether the shell file in host_user_shell actually exists & is executable

recentSshdRejections.title: Last N sshd journal lines mentioning this username (rejections, accepts)

likelyCause.title: One-line human-readable diagnosis of the most likely cause

nextActions.title: Ordered concrete remediation steps the caller can take

sourceRepo.description:
Public source repository for the daemon serving this report. Agents
can clone or webfetch this to grep for the exact code path that
generated a symptom — useful when the structured fields are
inconclusive (likely_cause == "no obvious host-side problem"…).

daemonVersion.description:
Daemon version (containarium binary) that generated this report.
Pair with source_repo to read code at the exact commit/tag.

DebugContainerResponse.title: DebugContainerResponse is the structured diagnostic report

DeleteContainerResponse.title: DeleteContainerResponse is the response from deleting a container

DeletePolicy.description:
DeletePolicy controls whether a container may be removed by the daemon's
UNATTENDED deletion paths (#284). It gates only the automated/bulk sweeps —
the ttlsweeper's auto-reap and `containarium prune`; a deliberate single-box
delete always succeeds regardless, so this is a guard against a "clean up
leaked boxes" sweep taking out a persistent box (e.g. a GitHub Actions
runner), not a deletion lock. Backed by the user.containarium.delete_policy
Incus config key (DeletePolicyKey / DeletePolicyProtected in pkg/core/incus).

 - DELETE_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED: Unprotected — eligible for prune + auto-reap (today's default; the
absent/empty config value maps here).
 - DELETE_POLICY_PROTECTED: Protected — skipped by the ttlsweeper auto-reap and `containarium prune`.
A deliberate single-box delete still removes it.

DeployAppRequest.title: DeployAppRequest deploys a new app or updates existing

name.description:
Deployment / container identity (the new container is named
"<name>-container", same identity scheme as `containarium create`).

gpu.description:
GPU device ID for passthrough (e.g. "0" or a PCI address). Required for
recipes with requires_gpu unless a GPU-capable backend is selected.

backendId.description: Target backend ID for placement (empty = primary backend).

pool.description: Pool selector for placement (empty = untagged backends only).

parameters.description: Overrides for the recipe's declared parameters (by name).

resourceOverrides.description: Optional overrides for the recipe's default resource limits.

labels.description:
Custom labels stamped on the provisioned container (forwarded to
CreateContainer). Lets a control plane attribute a recipe-deployed box to
a tenant/org the same way it does for a plain CreateContainer — without
labels, a cloud front-end that filters containers by label can't see a
recipe-deployed box. Same key/value rules as CreateContainerRequest.labels.

async.description:
async runs the recipe's post_start in the background: the RPC returns as
soon as the container is created (state CREATING) instead of blocking until
post_start finishes. Set this when the deploy is driven over a connection
with a request/idle timeout shorter than the post_start (e.g. a control
plane reaching a BYOC host through a peer-proxy): a recipe that pulls a
multi-GB image can exceed that timeout, which would otherwise abort the
deploy mid-pull. The CLI leaves this false so a human sees post_start's
result inline. The box becomes fully functional once the background
post_start completes; poll the container / its workspace access to detect
readiness.

url.description: Public URL of the first exposed port, if any.

DeployRecipeResponse.description: DeployRecipeResponse is the result of a recipe deployment.

connectionCount.title: Number of connections to this destination

bytesTotal.title: Total bytes transferred to/from this destination

DestinationStats.title: DestinationStats provides traffic statistics for a destination

already.description: True when the unit wasn't enabled and nothing was disabled.

root.description:
Optional: walk root. Defaults to the tenant's $HOME inside the
LXC when empty.

maxDepth.description:
Optional: directory depth cap. Default matches the agent-box
default (see internal/agentbox/compose.go defaultMaxDepth).

skip.description:
Optional: extra directory names to skip on the walk (additive
to the default skip set: node_modules, .git, etc.).

noSkip.description: Optional: bypass the default skip set entirely.

dir.description: Compose directory inside the LXC. Required.

force.description:
Re-install the systemd unit even if already enabled. Use after
changing the compose file.

dir.description: Absolute path inside the LXC that was enabled.

composeBin.description: The resolved compose binary inside the LXC.

already.description:
True when the unit was already enabled and no work was done
(with force=false). False when this call installed or refreshed
the unit.

message.description:
Operator-facing summary, set when there's something interesting
to say beyond the structured fields (e.g. "already enabled (use
force=true to refresh)").

joinToken.description:
The join token, format "<host_id>.<secret>", from the cloud's
`pool join` / `cloud enroll` one-liner.

driverToken.description:
driver_token is an admin JWT this host minted with its OWN daemon
jwt.secret (`containarium token generate --roles admin --secret-file
/etc/containarium/jwt.secret`). The cloud stores it sealed and replays
it to drive this host's daemon through the sentinel peer-proxy (BYOC
Option β, cloud #554). Optional: empty leaves the host enrolled but
not cloud-drivable. The host stays sovereign — it signs with its own
secret and rotates by re-enrolling.

ossBackendId.description:
oss_backend_id is the id this host's tunnel registered under at the
sentinel (its `pool join` spot-id) — what the sentinel `/peer/<id>/`
proxy keys on. The cloud records it to map a container's backend id
back to this host. Empty if not (yet) a peer-proxy-routed BYOC backend.

EnrollHostRequest.description:
EnrollHostRequest carries the single-use join token a BYO host redeems to
register itself with the cloud (BYO-compute report path). Unlike every other
ActuationService RPC this one is NOT host-bearer authed — the host row
doesn't exist yet; the cloud validates + single-use-redeems the token.

hostId.description:
The enrolled host's id. The host keeps using its join token as its
durable host-bearer for Heartbeat / ReportHostStatus.

resourceId.title: Resource identifier (container name, app ID, route domain)

Event.title: Event is the top-level event message sent to clients

EventType.description:
- EVENT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified event type (should not be used)
 - EVENT_TYPE_CONTAINER_CREATED: Container events (1-9)
Container was created
 - EVENT_TYPE_CONTAINER_DELETED: Container was deleted
 - EVENT_TYPE_CONTAINER_STARTED: Container was started
 - EVENT_TYPE_CONTAINER_STOPPED: Container was stopped
 - EVENT_TYPE_CONTAINER_STATE_CHANGED: Container state changed
 - EVENT_TYPE_APP_DEPLOYED: App events (10-19)
App was deployed
 - EVENT_TYPE_APP_DELETED: App was deleted
 - EVENT_TYPE_APP_STARTED: App was started
 - EVENT_TYPE_APP_STOPPED: App was stopped
 - EVENT_TYPE_APP_STATE_CHANGED: App state changed
 - EVENT_TYPE_ROUTE_ADDED: Network events (20-29)
Route was added
 - EVENT_TYPE_ROUTE_DELETED: Route was deleted
 - EVENT_TYPE_METRICS_UPDATE: System events (30-39)
Metrics update
 - EVENT_TYPE_TRAFFIC_UPDATE: Traffic events (40-49)
Traffic/connection update

True when a real KMS backend is active (backend != none and no
config error).

True when CONTAINARIUM_REQUIRE_ENVELOPE is set (legacy rows

hasn't succeeded yet (e.g. rate-limited with no cached value).

The decrypted plaintext value. Caller's responsibility to keep
safe — once it's on the wire, the daemon's encryption guarantee
ends.

GetSystemInfoResponse.title: GetSystemInfoResponse is the response from getting system information

currentVersion.description: Daemon version recorded when the upgrade was triggered.

completedAt.description: RFC3339 completion time when status is "completed" or "failed".

GetUpgradeStatusResponse.description:
GetUpgradeStatusResponse reports an upgrade's progress. Note: a local
self-upgrade restarts the daemon, dropping in-memory job state — after a
restart this returns status "unknown" and callers should compare the
backend's version in ListBackends instead. See #354.

token.description: The in-box auth proxy's per-box session token.

url.description:
Ready-to-embed bootstrap URL (https://<workspace-domain>/__ws_login?t=...);
loading it sets the session cookie and redirects to the workspace UI.

GetWorkspaceAccessResponse.description:
GetWorkspaceAccessResponse carries a bootstrap URL the console can load in an
iframe to sign the user into the in-box workspace proxy without a prompt.

HeartbeatRequest.description:
HeartbeatRequest is intentionally empty for v1. The host's identity
comes from the bearer; there's nothing else the cloud needs to
know to bump last_heartbeat_at. Future fields (current observed
state, host-side health flags) are additive.

receivedAt.description:
Server time when the heartbeat was recorded. Useful for hosts
that want to skew-correct their heartbeat scheduling against
the cloud's clock.

endedAt.title: Connection end time (null if still active)

HistoricalConnection.title: HistoricalConnection represents a persisted connection record

HostCapabilityCheck.description:
HostCapabilityCheck is one line of the `doctor` self-check: a named probe
with a pass/fail + human-readable detail. Defined locally here (same field
numbers as the cloud's host.proto message) so the vendored actuation proto
stays self-contained — wire-compatible without vendoring host.proto.

stackId.title: Stack or base script ID to install (e.g., "nodejs", "clamav")

InstallStackBody.title: InstallStackRequest is the request to install a stack on a running container

InstallStackResponse.title: InstallStackResponse is the response from installing a stack
