ListCollaboratorsResponse.title: ListCollaboratorsResponse is the response from listing collaborators

containers.title: List of containers matching the filter criteria

ListContainersResponse.title: ListContainersResponse is the response from listing containers

ListDefaultAlertRulesResponse.title: ListDefaultAlertRulesResponse returns the built-in default alert rules

ListStacksResponse.description: ListStacksResponse returns all configured software stacks.

sharedVolumesSupported.description:
True only when this backend has a CephFS pool and can serve shared
volumes. False on single-node ZFS hosts (today's default).

capabilityDetail.description:
Human-readable explanation (e.g. the detected pool, or why support
is unavailable).

dryRun.description:
When true, walk + verify every legacy row would round-trip but
issue no UPDATE.

batchSize.description: Rows scanned per page. 0 → server default (100).

maxRows.description: Cap on total rows processed in one call. 0 → unlimited.

targetBackendId.description:
Target backend ID — the peer daemon to migrate to. Must appear in
the source daemon's PeerPool. Trying to move to the local backend
is a no-op error.

maxIterations.description:
Maximum pre-copy delta iterations before forcing the cutover.
Each iteration copies the delta since the previous snapshot; with
each iteration the dirty set should shrink, reducing the final
cutover downtime. Range [0, 10]. Zero disables iterative delta
and goes straight to stop + full copy. Default 3.

deltaThresholdSeconds.description:
If a delta iteration completes in less than this many seconds,
the caller decides the dirty set is small enough — skip remaining
iterations and go straight to cutover. Range [1, 60]. Default 5.

stateful.description:
When true, attempt CRIU-based live migration via `incus move
--stateful` — preserves process memory state and open sockets,
sub-second downtime even on active workloads. Requires CRIU
installed on both source and target, and is not supported for
containers running podman/docker-in-LXC. Defaults to false
(stop-and-copy) which is more reliable.

MoveContainerBody.description:
MoveContainerRequest is the source-side request to migrate a
container to a peer daemon.

newIpAddress.description: The container's new IP address on the target daemon, after start.

targetBackendId.description: Backend the container now lives on (target_backend_id from req).

iterationsRun.description:
Total iterations performed (initial copy + N deltas + final).
Helpful for tuning max_iterations on subsequent moves of similar
workloads.

downtimeSeconds.description:
Total wall-clock seconds the container was unreachable during the
cutover (between stop on source and start on target with route
updated). With pre-copy + ZFS this should be < 5s; on dir-pool or
active workloads it may be tens of seconds. -1 if stateful=true
succeeded and there was no observable downtime.

preset.title: Preset type (if using a predefined rule set)

NetworkACL.title: NetworkACL represents firewall rules for a container/app

NetworkEdge.title: NetworkEdge represents a connection between nodes

ipAddress.title: Primary IP address assigned to the container

macAddress.title: MAC address of the container's network interface

NetworkInfo.title: NetworkInfo contains network configuration for a container

NetworkNode.title: NetworkNode represents a node in the network topology

cidr.description:
Destination CIDR to block (e.g. "1.2.3.4/32", "10.0.0.0/8"). Required.
IPv4 only (Phase A parses IPv4); a host IP is just a /32.

port.description:
Optional destination port to scope the block (0 = any port). Lets a rule
target only the vulnerable service (e.g. a Redis CVE on 6379) without
blocking unrelated traffic to the same host.

proto.description: Optional IP protocol to scope the block: "tcp" | "udp" | "" (any).

note.description:
Operator note — typically the CVE id this rule virtual-patches. Echoed on
get/list and carried into the audit detail.

expiresAt.description:
Optional RFC3339 expiry (e.g. "2026-07-01T00:00:00Z"). A rule past its
expiry is not installed into the kernel, so a virtual patch auto-removes
once the real fix is confirmed. Empty = no expiry.

NetworkPolicyDenyRule.description:
NetworkPolicyDenyRule is one virtual-patch block rule (#660). Traffic from a
tenant's container to the destination is denied — dropped in ENFORCE mode,
audited (action network_policy.virtual_patch) in every mode. Deny beats the
egress allow-list.

name.description:
Unique operator label, typically the CVE id (e.g. "CVE-2024-1234"). The
identity for upsert/delete.

pattern.description:
The cleartext bytes to substring-match in the inbound payload, 1..32 bytes
(the BPF SIG_MAX_LEN). Matched as raw bytes; a longer pattern is rejected.

enabled.description:
Whether this signature is active. Default true; a disabled signature is
stored but not loaded into the kernel scan.

id.description:
Assigned signature id (output only), echoed in the audit on a match. Operator
signatures get ids in a reserved high range so they never collide with the
built-in set's low ids.

NetworkPolicySignature.description:
NetworkPolicySignature is one operator-managed cleartext exploit signature
(#661 Tier 2, PR-B). Unlike deny rules these are GLOBAL (fleet-wide), not
tenant-scoped — an exploit pattern is matched against every scanned container's
inbound payload. They augment the daemon's curated built-in set.

NetworkTopology.title: NetworkTopology represents the complete network visualization

OSType.description:
- OS_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified OS type (defaults to Ubuntu 24.04)
 - OS_TYPE_UBUNTU_2404: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS
 - OS_TYPE_ROCKY_9: Rocky Linux 9 (RHEL 9 rebuild, for dev/test)
 - OS_TYPE_RHEL_9: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 (for production, requires subscription)
 - OS_TYPE_WINDOWS_2022: Windows Server 2022 (runs as QEMU/KVM VM, RDP access)
OSType.title: OSType represents the operating system type for a container

PassthroughRoute.title: PassthroughRoute represents a direct TCP/UDP port forwarding rule

PatchNetworkPolicyDenyRulesRequest.description:
PatchNetworkPolicyDenyRulesRequest atomically mutates a tenant's virtual-patch
deny rules (#660): the server reads the current rules, removes any whose CIDR
is in remove_cidrs, adds/replaces the `add` rules (deny rules are keyed by
CIDR — an add for an existing CIDR replaces it), re-normalizes, and writes
back under a lock, so concurrent edits don't lose updates. The allow-policy
(egress, mode, intra, metadata) is untouched. The normalized stored policy is
echoed back.

PentestConfig.title: PentestConfig returns the current pentest configuration

target.title: Target that was scanned (URL, IP:port, domain)

evidence.title: Evidence or raw output supporting the finding

firstScanRunId.title: Scan run ID that first found this

lastScanRunId.title: Scan run ID that last saw this

resolvedAt.title: When resolved (ISO 8601, empty if still open)

PentestFinding.title: PentestFinding represents a single security finding

modules.title: Comma-separated list of modules that were run

completedCount.title: Number of targets that finished processing

containerName.description:
Container the scan was scoped to. Empty = cluster-wide scan
(the historical/scheduled behavior). Set when an operator triggers
an on-demand scan against a specific container.

PentestScanRun.title: PentestScanRun represents a single penetration test scan execution

database.description: Logical database to dump or restore into. Required.

user.description: Postgres role. Defaults to "postgres" when empty.

password.description:
Password for the role. Optional (empty for trust/peer auth or when a
PGPASSFILE is configured in the container). Never logged.

host.description: Host as seen from *inside* the container. Defaults to "127.0.0.1".

PgConnection.description:
PgConnection carries the connection parameters pg_dump / pg_restore use
*inside the container*. Defaults target a per-container local Postgres
reached over loopback. db_password is never logged and is passed to the
child process via the PGPASSWORD environment variable, not argv.

backendId.description:
Backend to profile. Empty = the local/primary daemon's own host; a peer id
forwards to that peer (which profiles itself).

skipGpu.description:
Skip the GPU passthrough probe (which creates+deletes a throwaway LXC and
can take ~30s). The profile then records gpu_available=false. Useful on a
backend known to be CPU-only.

ProfileBackendRequest.description:
ProfileBackendRequest asks a backend to (re)record its capability profile:
read system info, run the GPU passthrough probe and the bounded
CPU/memory micro-benchmark, derive the measured class, and persist it. See
#681.

ProfileBackendResponse.description: ProfileBackendResponse returns the freshly recorded capability profile.

name.description:
Stable name/path identifying the program object (e.g. the configured BPF
object path).

digest.description: Hex-encoded digest of the program object bytes.

ProgramDigest.description: ProgramDigest is one loaded in-kernel program object's identity + digest.

Protocol.description:
- PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified protocol (should not be used)
 - PROTOCOL_TCP: TCP protocol
 - PROTOCOL_UDP: UDP protocol
 - PROTOCOL_ICMP: ICMP protocol
Protocol.title: Protocol represents the network protocol of a connection

containerName.description:
Name of the container this route forwards to (the box behind the
upstream IP), e.g. "cld-abc123". Resolved from the route record or by
reverse-lookup of container_ip. Distinct from app_name (the display
name): a multi-tenant control plane keys its route reconciler on the
container, so it needs the box identity, not just the display label.

ProxyRoute.title: ProxyRoute represents a DNS/domain to container mapping

image.description: OCI image run via `podman run` inside the LXC (e.g. "ollama/ollama").

requiresGpu.title: When true, the deploy must specify a GPU

resources.description: Default resource limits for the container.

ports.description: Ports to expose publicly after the app is running.

volumes.description: Persistent volumes to create and mount.

env.description: Static environment variables passed to the app.

Deploy-time parameters (with defaults), surfaced to UIs and exported to
post_start as CONTAINARIUM_PARAM_<NAME>.

Shell commands run inside the container after it is up (e.g. the
`podman run ...` that starts the app, plus model warm-up). Same trust
level as a stack's post_install: these ship in-tree and are reviewed.

Recipe.description:
Recipe is a declarative definition of a GPU/app workload that Containarium
can provision as a new dedicated container. The container is an LXC system
container; the recipe's image runs inside it via Podman (post_start),
mirroring how the kubeflow stack runs k3s inside an LXC.

name.description:
Parameter key (e.g. "model"). Env var seen by post_start is
CONTAINARIUM_PARAM_<upper(name)>.

type.description:
"string", "password", "number", or "boolean". Controls UI rendering;
the value is always carried as a string.

default.description: Fallback value if the deployer leaves it blank.

RecipeParam.description:
RecipeParam describes a deploy-time input the recipe accepts. Mirrors
stacks.StackParameter. Values are exported to post_start commands as
CONTAINARIUM_PARAM_<NAME> environment variables.

containerPort.title: Port the app listens on inside the container (e.g. 11434)

subdomain.description:
Subdomain to expose it under (e.g. "ollama"); combined with the
deployment name and the daemon's base domain to form the public host.

RecipePort.description: RecipePort maps a container port to a public subdomain.

RecipeResources.description: RecipeResources holds default resource limits for a recipe's container.

path.title: Mount path inside the app container (e.g. "/root/.ollama")

RecipeVolume.description: RecipeVolume is a named persistent volume mounted into the app container.

RefreshSecretsBody.description:
RefreshSecretsRequest re-stamps the LXC's environment.<NAME>
config keys from the current secret DB state for the tenant. Used
after rotation when the operator wants the next process exec'd in
the LXC to see the new value without restarting the container.

Running processes in the container do NOT pick up the new env
(Linux environments are inherited at fork time, not refreshed).
For that, the tenant restarts the container or starts a new
process.

stamped.description:
Count of env vars actually stamped (matches the number of
secrets owned by the tenant at refresh time).

refreshToken.description:
The current refresh token. Must validate via
ValidateRefreshToken (signature + iss + aud + exp +
tt == "refresh") AND not be in the revocation list.

RefreshTokenRequest.description:
Phase 1.6 part B — exchange a refresh token for a new
(access, refresh) pair. Rotates the refresh token:
the prior jti is revoked on success, so each refresh
token is single-use. Replay = denied + audit signal.

accessToken.description:
New short-lived access token, ready to use as
Authorization: Bearer.

refreshToken.description:
New refresh token. Save this — the one you just sent
in is now revoked and cannot be exchanged again.

accessTokenExpiresAt.description:
Unix-seconds expiry of the access token, for clients
that want to refresh proactively before exp.

refreshTokenExpiresAt.description: Unix-seconds expiry of the refresh token.

RemediatePentestFindingBody.description:
RemediatePentestFindingRequest requests remediation of a Trivy finding by
upgrading the OS package that contains the vulnerable binary.

RemediatePentestFindingResponse.description: RemediatePentestFindingResponse returns the result of the remediation attempt.

RemoveCollaboratorResponse.title: RemoveCollaboratorResponse is the response from removing a collaborator

totalKeys.title: Total number of SSH keys remaining

RemoveSSHKeyResponse.title: RemoveSSHKeyResponse is the response from removing an SSH key

state.description:
Observed state. Must match one of the AssignmentState* values
(pending / active / stopping / stopped). Empty rejected with
InvalidArgument; values outside the enum also InvalidArgument.

observedAt.description:
When the host observed this state. Optional — defaults to the
server's clock at write time when zero. The server stamps its
own updated_at regardless; observed_at carries the host's view
for skew analysis but isn't currently used to gate writes.

ReportContainerStateBody.description:
ReportContainerStateRequest is a host's push of its current
observation about a container it's been assigned.

receivedAt.description: Server time when the report was recorded.

driverToken.description:
driver_token is a freshly-minted admin JWT from this host's own jwt.secret
(#557). If non-empty the cloud reseals it and overwrites the stored driver
token, keeping the cloud-stored credential from expiring after 30 days.

ReportHostStatusRequest.description:
ReportHostStatusRequest is a BYO host's push of its self-measured
capability profile + `doctor` self-check. Host-bearer authed; the host id
comes from the bearer, never the body.

cpu.title: New CPU limit (e.g., "4", "2-4") - empty means no change

memory.title: New memory limit (e.g., "8GB", "4096MB") - empty means no change

disk.title:
New disk size (e.g., "100GB") - empty means no change
Note: Can only increase, cannot shrink

ResizeContainerBody.title: ResizeContainerRequest is the request to resize container resources

gpu.description:
Deprecated: single-GPU field. Prefer `gpus` for one-or-many. When `gpus`
is non-empty it takes precedence and this field is ignored; when `gpus`
is empty a non-empty `gpu` is treated as a single-element list.
gpu.title: GPU device ID for passthrough (e.g., "0" for first GPU, PCI address, or empty for none)

gpus.description:
GPU device IDs for passthrough — one entry per GPU to attach (each is a
device index like "0"/"1" or a PCI address like "0000:0b:00.0"). Empty
means no GPU. Supersedes the singular `gpu` field.

ResourceLimits.title: ResourceLimits defines resource constraints for a container

ResourceType.title: ResourceType identifies which resource type an event pertains to

connection.description:
Connection parameters for pg_restore inside the target container.
If connection.database is empty, the backup record's database is
used (restore in place).

clean.description:
Pass --clean --if-exists to pg_restore (drop objects before
recreating). Off by default so a restore into a fresh database
does not error on missing objects.

RestoreBackupBody.description:
RestoreBackupRequest streams a stored dump back into a container's
database via pg_restore. Destructive: with `clean` set it drops and
recreates objects before loading.

jti.description: The jti claim of the revoked token.

expiresAt.description:
RFC3339 timestamp of the token's original `exp` claim.
Used as the cleanup horizon (rows past this can no
longer authenticate anyway, so the daemon prunes
them eventually).

revokedAt.description:
RFC3339 timestamp of when the revocation row was
first inserted. Operators correlating an incident
window use this column.

reason.description:
Free-form reason recorded at revoke time (e.g.
"operator_revoke", "refresh_rotation", "leak_2026_X").

Revocation.description:
Revocation is one row of the revocation list, surfaced
by the admin listing path. The plaintext token itself is
not in the table — only the jti is.

jti.description:
The `jti` claim of the token to revoke. Required.
Operators get the jti either from the audit trail
(every authenticated request logs its jti) or by
decoding the token they want to kill.

reason.description:
Optional free-form reason recorded in the revocation
table for forensics. Examples: "user_logout",
"compromise_2026_05_20", "operator_revoke".

expiresAt.description:
Optional expiry hint — when the token's exp claim is.
Used as the cleanup horizon for the row. If omitted,
the daemon falls back to the configured max token
lifetime so the row eventually prunes itself.

RevokeTokenRequest.description: RevokeTokenRequest names a token to invalidate.

newlyRevoked.description:
True when a new revocation row was inserted; false when
the jti was already revoked (idempotent — the existing
revocation reason is preserved).

message.description: Human-readable confirmation, intended for CLI output.

RouteProtocol.description:
- ROUTE_PROTOCOL_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified protocol (defaults to HTTP)
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_HTTP: HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 (standard web traffic) - for proxy routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_GRPC: gRPC (requires HTTP/2 transport) - for proxy routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_TCP: TCP passthrough - for passthrough routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_UDP: UDP passthrough - for passthrough routes
 - ROUTE_PROTOCOL_TLS_PASSTHROUGH: TLS passthrough - SNI-based routing without TLS termination (for mTLS gRPC)
RouteProtocol.title: RouteProtocol represents the protocol type for a route

backendId.description: Target backend/pool placement, same scheme as recipes.

inputJson.description: JSON task input matching the skill's agent_card input schema.

RunAgentSkillBody.description:
RunAgentSkillRequest launches a single skill in its box and sends it one
task (no peers). Useful for testing a skill in isolation.

container.description: The box the agent ran in.

artifactJson.description: JSON artifact matching the skill's output schema.

inputJson.description: JSON input for the crew's entry skill(s).

status.title: Job status: pending, running, completed, failed

retryCount.title: Number of retry attempts so far

errorMessage.title: Error message if the job failed

backendId.title: Backend ID identifying which node owns this job

ScanJob.title: ScanJob represents a queued ClamAV scan job

name.description:
Secret name. Matches `^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$` (Linux env-var convention,
up to 128 chars).

version.description:
Monotonic version that bumps on every SetSecret for the same
(username, name). Useful for rotation diagnostics ("LXC env says
version=3, DB says version=4 → restart needed").

delivery.description:
Phase 4.3 — how the secret is delivered into the container.
"env" (default): stamped as environment.<NAME>=<value> on the LXC,
visible to every process in the container via /proc/<pid>/environ.
"file": written to a per-container tmpfs at /run/secrets/<NAME>,
file mode 0440 root:<tenant>. Same tenant isolation, narrower
in-container access surface.
"compose": written to a shared dotenv file at
/run/containarium/secrets.env (mode 0400, tmpfs) that nested
docker / docker-compose apps consume via `env_file:` — they don't
inherit the LXC environment (the same gap OTel solved). Single-line
values only; rejected at set-time otherwise. See
docs/security/SECRETS-ENV-VAR-RISK.md.

SecretMetadata.description:
SecretMetadata is the public-safe view of a stored secret.
`value` is never returned by `ListSecrets` — only per-name `GetSecret`.

hashAlgorithm.description: Hash algorithm used for every component digest below (e.g. "sha256").

binaryDigest.description: Hex-encoded digest of the running daemon binary on disk.

programDigests.description:
Hex-encoded digests of the loaded in-kernel network-policy program
object(s), in stable (sorted) order. Empty when no program object is
configured on this backend.

configDigest.description:
Hex-encoded digest of the canonicalized policy/config state (the
integrity-relevant daemon configuration + active network-policy posture).

measurementDigest.description:
Hex-encoded digest of the canonical measurement bytes that were signed
(binds all components together). The signature below covers exactly these
bytes.

signature.description: Signature over the canonical measurement bytes, base64-encoded.

signatureAlgorithm.description:
Signature scheme used (e.g. "ecdsa-p256-sha256"). Empty when the daemon had
no identity key available and the measurement is unsigned (signature empty).

tpmBacked.description:
Whether the signature was produced by a TPM-backed key. False = the
software identity key (sentinel-issued peer leaf) signed it.

signed.description:
Whether an identity key was available to sign. False means the measurement
is present but unsigned (no peer PKI bootstrapped yet); the control plane
treats an unsigned measurement as unverifiable, not as tamper.

signingCertPem.description:
PEM of the signing certificate (the peer leaf), so the control plane can
verify the signature against the sentinel CA chain without a side lookup.
Empty when unsigned.

measuredAt.description: RFC3339 timestamp the measurement was taken.

daemonVersion.description: Daemon version that produced the measurement.

SelfMeasurement.description:
SelfMeasurement is a backend's signed integrity attestation, emitted by the
daemon on its heartbeat. It hashes the daemon's own binary, the loaded
in-kernel network-policy program object(s), and the policy/config state, then
signs the canonical measurement with the node's identity key (the
sentinel-issued peer leaf key reused from the peer-PKI plumbing; TPM-backed
when a TPM is present, software-signed otherwise). The control plane reads
this through the backend surface to detect tampering of a backend's control
plane out of band of the backend's own reporting. The node-side half only:
the control plane's verification/quarantine logic lives elsewhere. See #683.

fromSkillId.description: The calling skill (for attribution + the future allowed_peers check).

inputJson.description: JSON task input matching the peer's agent_card input schema.

SendAgentTaskBody.description:
SendAgentTaskRequest delegates a task to a running peer agent. The peer is
addressed by its agent_card.id (or skill id). In Phase 2 the daemon will
reject a send to a peer not in the caller's allowed_peers; in Phase 1 the
field set is established and the send is best-effort over A2A.

traceId.description:
The trace id this hop was audited under (generated if the request omitted
one). Lets a caller chain subsequent hops under the same id.

deletePolicy.description:
Target policy. DELETE_POLICY_PROTECTED protects the box from the ttlsweeper
auto-reap and `containarium prune`; DELETE_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED clears the
protection (back to the default — eligible for both).

SetContainerDeletePolicyBody.description:
SetContainerDeletePolicyRequest sets or clears a container's protection from
the daemon's automated/bulk deletion paths (#284).

deletePolicy.description: The delete policy now in effect on the container.

SetContainerDeletePolicyResponse.description: SetContainerDeletePolicyResponse reports the committed delete policy.

durationSeconds.description:
Duration from now() at which the container should be deleted.
duration_seconds == 0 clears any existing TTL (the container
persists indefinitely). duration_seconds > 0 sets ttl_expires_at
to now() + duration. Capped at 604800 (7 days) — larger values
return InvalidArgument. The cap mirrors the CLI's 168h ceiling
(see containarium ttl set, PR #297) and bounds the worst-case
forgotten-box cost.

SetContainerTTLBody.description:
SetContainerTTLRequest schedules or clears a container's auto-delete
time. The daemon's ttlsweeper goroutine consumes ttl_expires_at and
force-deletes once the wall clock crosses it.

ttlExpiresAt.description:
The wall-clock deletion time the daemon committed. Unset when
the caller passed duration_seconds == 0 (i.e. TTL cleared).

SetContainerTTLResponse.description: SetContainerTTLResponse reports the new TTL state.

policy.description: The stored policy (echoed back in its normalized form).

signature.title: stored form, with the assigned id

name.description:
Secret name. Server enforces ^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$ + 128 char max
(per docs/SECRETS-MANAGEMENT-DESIGN.md decision #1).

value.description:
Plaintext value the daemon will encrypt with its master key
before persisting. Server enforces a 64 KiB hard cap
(decision #2). Never logged.

delivery.description:
Phase 4.3 — delivery mode for the secret. Accepts "env"
(default; same behavior as pre-4.3), "file" (per-secret tmpfs
/run/secrets/<NAME>), or "compose" (shared dotenv at
/run/containarium/secrets.env for docker-compose `env_file:`;
single-line values only). Unknown values are rejected at the
API boundary.

SetSecretRequest.description:
SetSecretRequest creates or updates a tenant secret. Idempotent —
repeated calls with the same (username, name) bump the version and
replace the value.

StackInfo.description: StackInfo describes a single software stack.

name.description:
Parameter key. The env var seen by install scripts is
CONTAINARIUM_STACK_<name>.

label.description: Human-readable label shown in the web UI.

description.description: Optional helper text shown below the field.

default.description: Default value if the user leaves the field blank.

StackParameter.description:
StackParameter describes an input the stack accepts at install time.
Returned by ListStacks so the web UI can render appropriate form fields.

waitForReady.description:
When true, the server blocks until the container's primary TCP
port (from its route record) is accepting connections, or
ready_timeout_seconds elapses. If the container has no route the
probe is skipped. Used by `containarium wake` to give callers an
"actually serving" signal rather than just "incus says started".

readyTimeoutSeconds.description:
Timeout for the readiness probe in seconds. Zero falls back to
the server-side default (30s). Ignored when wait_for_ready is
false.

StartContainerBody.title: StartContainerRequest is the request to start a container

readyTimedOut.description:
True when wait_for_ready was set but the readiness probe timed
out before the primary port accepted. The container is started
either way — the caller can retry the probe or accept the start
as best-effort.

StartContainerResponse.title: StartContainerResponse is the response from starting a container

stack.description:
Single-stack status. Same shape as one entry from
DiscoverResponse.stacks.

force.title: Force stop (kill) instead of graceful shutdown

timeout.title: Timeout in seconds for graceful shutdown (default: 30)

StopContainerBody.title: StopContainerRequest is the request to stop a container

StopContainerResponse.title: StopContainerResponse is the response from stopping a container

backendId.title: Backend ID (e.g., "tunnel-node-a-gpu") — set when returned from peer

otelCollectorEndpoint.description:
OTLP collector endpoint (e.g., "http://10.0.3.5:4318") that
monitoring=true containers ship app telemetry to. Empty when the
core OTel collector isn't provisioned. Advertised here so a tenant
or agent can discover where to point OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT —
in particular for docker-in-LXC apps that don't inherit the
env-stamped value. See #370.

daemonVersion.description:
Containarium daemon version of this backend (e.g. "0.21.0"), so the
fleet's running versions + drift are visible via get_system_info and
/v1/backends without SSHing each host. See #354.

SystemInfo.title: SystemInfo contains information about the host system

TestWebhookRequest.title: TestWebhookRequest triggers a test notification to the configured webhook

success.title: Whether the test notification was sent successfully

statusCode.title: HTTP status code from the webhook endpoint

TestWebhookResponse.title: TestWebhookResponse returns the result of the test

enabled.description:
Desired state. true → write user.containarium.auto_sleep_enabled
= "true". false → write "false" (the threshold key is left in
place so a re-enable picks up the prior value).

idleThresholdMinutes.description:
Idle minutes before Phase 2 would stop the container. Ignored
when enabled=false; 0 means "use existing key or fall back to 15".

ToggleAutoSleepBody.description:
ToggleAutoSleepRequest opts a container into (or out of) auto-sleep
tracking. The flag and threshold are stored as Incus user.* config
keys; the actual sleep tick (Phase 2) and HTTP wake (Phase 3) are
separate work. This RPC only writes the metadata.

autoSleepEnabled.description: The effective auto_sleep_enabled state after the toggle.

idleThresholdMinutes.description: The effective idle_threshold_minutes value (15 if defaulted).

ToggleAutoSleepResponse.description: ToggleAutoSleepResponse reports the effective auto-sleep state.

enabled.description:
Desired state. true → stamp env vars + restart. false → unset
env vars + restart so the SDK falls back to its buffer+drop
default.

ToggleMonitoringBody.description:
ToggleMonitoringRequest enables / disables OTel app telemetry on
an existing container. The four OTEL_* env vars are stamped (or
unset) and the container is restarted so the new env reaches the
app process.

message.description:
Operator-facing summary of what changed (e.g. "monitoring
enabled — container restarted").

monitoringEnabled.description:
The effective monitoring_enabled state of the container after
the toggle (mirrors Container.monitoring_enabled in subsequent
GetContainer responses).

ToggleMonitoringResponse.description: ToggleMonitoringResponse reports the new monitoring state.

bytesSent.title: Total bytes sent in this interval

bytesReceived.title: Total bytes received in this interval

connectionCount.title: Number of connections in this interval

TrafficAggregate.title: TrafficAggregate provides time-series aggregated traffic data

- TRAFFIC_DIRECTION_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified direction

 - TRAFFIC_DIRECTION_EGRESS: Traffic leaving the container
TrafficDirection.title: TrafficDirection indicates the direction of traffic relative to the container

TrafficEvent.title: TrafficEvent represents a real-time connection event

- TRAFFIC_EVENT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified event type

 - TRAFFIC_EVENT_TYPE_UPDATE: Connection state or counters updated

TrafficEventType.title: TrafficEventType represents the type of traffic event

containerName.title: Container name to scan (empty = scan all running user containers)

scannedCount.title: Number of containers queued for scanning

modules.title: Optional: comma-separated list of modules to run (empty = all enabled)

containerName.description:
Optional: container to scope the scan to. Empty = scan the whole
cluster (every active route + every running user container), the
historical default. Set to scope a single container's routes and
package surface for an on-demand operator scan.

backendId.description: Backend to upgrade. Empty = the local/primary daemon's own host.

force.description: Upgrade even if the sentinel-served binary already matches the running one.

TriggerUpgradeRequest.description:
TriggerUpgradeRequest asks a backend to upgrade its daemon binary now instead
of waiting for the periodic auto-update tick. The daemon pulls the binary the
sentinel currently serves (SHA-verified), smoke-tests it, swaps atomically
(keeping the previous binary as .old), and restarts. See #354 Phase B.

upgradeId.description: Opaque id for polling via GetUpgradeStatus.

status.description:
Status at acknowledgement time: "in_progress" (started) or "noop"
(already up to date and force=false).

currentVersion.description: The daemon version running when the upgrade was triggered.

message.description: Human-readable detail (e.g. why it was a noop).

TriggerUpgradeResponse.description:
TriggerUpgradeResponse acknowledges that an upgrade was started. The upgrade
runs asynchronously (the daemon restarts), so callers poll GetUpgradeStatus
or watch the backend's version in ListBackends for the outcome. See #354.

containerName.description:
Optional: container to scope the scan to. Empty = scan every
exposed route, the historical default. Set to scope a single
container's routes for an on-demand operator scan.

UpdateAlertRuleBody.title: UpdateAlertRuleRequest updates an existing alert rule

UpdateAlertRuleResponse.title: UpdateAlertRuleResponse returns the updated alert rule

webhookUrl.title: Webhook URL for alert notifications (empty string to disable)

generateWebhookSecret.title: When true, the server generates a new HMAC signing secret for webhook payloads

success.title: Whether the configuration was applied successfully

webhookSecret.title: The generated webhook secret (returned only once when generate_webhook_secret=true)

preset.title: Use a preset (mutually exclusive with custom rules)

ingressRules.title: Custom ingress rules (only if preset is CUSTOM)

egressRules.title: Custom egress rules (only if preset is CUSTOM)

UpdateContainerACLBody.title: UpdateContainerACLRequest updates firewall rules for a DevBox container

targetIp.title: New target IP (optional, empty means no change)

targetPort.title: New target port (optional, 0 means no change)

UpdatePassthroughRouteBody.title: UpdatePassthroughRouteRequest updates an existing passthrough route

targetIp.title: New target container IP address (optional, empty means no change)

targetPort.title: New target port (optional, 0 means no change)

protocol.title: Protocol type (HTTP or gRPC, defaults to HTTP)

active.title: Enable or disable the route (optional, only updated if set)

UpdateRouteBody.title: UpdateRouteRequest updates an existing proxy route

backendId.description: Backend to validate. Empty = the local/primary daemon's own host.

pci.description:
Specific GPU PCI address (e.g. "0000:01:00.0"). Empty = pass through all
GPUs. Prefer PCI over a DRM index — it's stable across kernel upgrades.

ValidateGPURequest.description:
ValidateGPURequest asks a backend to prove GPU passthrough works from inside
an LXC. See #316.

ValidateGPUResponse.description:
ValidateGPUResponse reports whether the GPU is usable from inside an LXC on
the backend: the daemon launches a throwaway nvidia.runtime LXC, runs
nvidia-smi inside, tears it down, and returns the parsed model + driver.

pool.description: CephFS storage pool backing the volume.

contentType.description: Incus content type — always "filesystem" for a shared volume.

attachments.description: Containers this volume is currently attached to.

Volume.description: Volume is a CephFS custom (filesystem) volume.

container.description: Container (tenant) the volume is mounted into.

VolumeAttachment.description: VolumeAttachment is one container a volume is mounted into.

WebhookDelivery.title: WebhookDelivery represents a single webhook delivery attempt

drained.description:
Container names that drained gracefully within the window. Empty unless
the request set drain. See #682.

forceStopped.description:
Container names that were force-stopped because the bounded window was
exhausted before they stopped gracefully.

drainWindowExceeded.description:
True when the graceful budget was exhausted and the backend fell back to
force-stopping the remaining workloads.

failed.description:
Container name → error for guests that could NOT be stopped even after the
force fallback. A non-empty map means the host is NOT fully reclaimed —
the control plane must not treat those guests' resources as freed.

WithdrawCapacityResponse.description: WithdrawCapacityResponse confirms the backend is no longer advertising.

firstScanRunId.title: Scan run ID that first found this

lastScanRunId.title: Scan run ID that last saw this

resolvedAt.title: When resolved (ISO 8601, empty if still open)

ZapAlert.title: ZapAlert represents a single ZAP alert (finding)

zapAvailable.title: Whether ZAP is installed and available

ZapConfig.title: ZapConfig returns the current ZAP configuration

completedCount.title: Number of targets that finished processing

containerName.description:
Container the scan was scoped to. Empty = cluster-wide scan
(every exposed route), the historical/scheduled default.

ZapScanRun.title: ZapScanRun represents a single ZAP scan execution

allowIntraTenant.description:
Allow container↔container traffic within the same tenant. When false, even
same-tenant peers can't reach each other.

egressDomains.description:
Allowed egress domains (e.g. "api.github.com"); the daemon resolves these
to CIDRs on a refresh loop and folds them into the egress allow set.

mode.description: Enforcement mode. Unspecified is treated as LOG_ONLY in Phase A.

allowMetadata.description:
Allow reaching the cloud metadata service (169.254.169.254). Default false:
the metadata IP is denied even if egress_cidrs/egress_domains would
otherwise cover it (deny-beats-allow for this one sensitive IP, since it
hands out cloud credentials). Set true only for a tenant that legitimately
needs instance metadata. #315 Phase D.

source.description:
Source identifies who authored this policy: "" / "cli" for an operator via
`containarium network-policy set`, "cloud" for one synced from a cloud
control plane (#354). The cloud-actuation reconciler converges only its own
("cloud") policies — so removing a policy cloud-side clears it on the host
without clobbering an operator's CLI-authored policy. Informational on
get/list.

denyRules.description:
Virtual-patch deny rules (#660). Each blocks traffic to a destination
CIDR (optionally scoped to a port/proto) and is evaluated BEFORE the
egress allow-list: deny beats allow, the same way the metadata IP does.
Use to "virtually patch" a known-vulnerable destination/service until the
real upstream fix ships — instant, in-kernel, zero downtime. A rule whose
expires_at is in the past is dropped at compile time, so the patch
self-removes once the fix lands.

containarium.v1.NetworkPolicyMode.description:
NetworkPolicyMode controls whether a tenant's network policy is enforced or
only observed. Phase A ships log_only; Phase B flips to enforce. See
docs/security/NETWORK-ISOLATION-DESIGN.md (#315).

 - NETWORK_POLICY_MODE_UNSPECIFIED: treated as LOG_ONLY in Phase A
 - NETWORK_POLICY_MODE_LOG_ONLY: observe + audit denied flows; drop nothing
 - NETWORK_POLICY_MODE_ENFORCE: actually drop denied flows
